subject: [PATCH] mm/nodemask: Correctly describe nodemask operation return types

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer SeongJae Park suggested converting the comment block to kernel-doc comments using modern documentation tools, as it was introduced before the git era and may become outdated in the future.
reviews.raw_body:
I feel like this comment block might be easy to be outdated in future.  This
comment block is introduced before the git era.  I think this was the best and
great way at that time.  But now we have modern documentation tools like
kernel-doc.  I'm wondering if it makes sense to convert this comment block to
kernel-doc comments in long term, after this patch is merged.  If we sure these
APIs are quite stable and expect no many changes in future, such conversion may
not make sense, though.


Thanks,
SJ

[...]

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch updates the documentation in nodemask.h to accurately describe the return types of various nodemask operations, which were previously changed from returning int to bool. Additionally, it fixes a typo and corrects a callsite in mempolicy.c that was not updated during the conversion.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the nodemask operation return types need updating and agrees that future changes to these functions should also migrate to bool return types.
reviews.raw_body:
I agree with this. I'm not entirely too sure if we expect too many changes
in this section, but I could always be surprised : -) I would say that even
in 2022 (when the nodemask function changes happened) not many people would
have expected changes to this otherwise stable set of functions.

So maybe the next changes to the function should also do the migration!

Thanks again, SJ! I hope you have a great day,
Joshua

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch updates the documentation in nodemask.h to accurately describe the return types of various nodemask operations, which were previously changed from returning int to bool. Additionally, it fixes a typo and corrects a callsite in mempolicy.c that was not updated during the conversion.
